REMEDIES.name: Cold
REMEDIES.summary: A warm decoction to ease cold symptoms.
steps: Add all ingredients to about 100 ml of water.
Boil for 5 minutes.
Strain and let it cool slightly.
Sip while warm.

REMEDIES.name: Cough
REMEDIES.summary: A soothing liquorice decoction to calm a cough.
steps: Add all ingredients to about 100 ml of water.
Boil for 5 minutes.
Strain and let it cool slightly.
Sip while warm.

REMEDIES.name: Blood Thinner
REMEDIES.summary: A warm drink taken after dinner.
steps: Add all ingredients to about 100 ml of water.
Boil for 5 minutes.
Strain and let it cool slightly.
Sip while warm.

REMEDIES.name: Upper Respiratory Congestion
REMEDIES.summary: A honey blend for chest and sinus congestion.
steps: Measure out the ingredients.
Mix thoroughly with 8 scoops of honey.
Take the mixture directly.

REMEDIES.name: Constipation
REMEDIES.summary: The classic Triphala blend, taken cold.
steps: Add the ingredients to 50 ml of water.
Stir well.
Drink it.

REMEDIES.name: Fever (above 102°F)
REMEDIES.summary: A Giloy decoction for high fever.
warn: A fever above 102°F, or any fever that persists, needs medical attention.
steps: Add all ingredients to about 100 ml of water.
Boil for 5 minutes.
Strain and let it cool slightly.
Sip while warm.

REMEDIES.name: Headache
REMEDIES.summary: A warm spiced decoction for headaches.
steps: Add all ingredients to about 100 ml of water.
Boil for 5 minutes.
Strain and let it cool slightly.
Sip while warm.

REMEDIES.name: Indigestion
REMEDIES.summary: A carminative decoction with rock salt.
steps: Add all ingredients to about 100 ml of water.
Boil for 5 minutes.
Strain and let it cool slightly.
Sip while warm.

REMEDIES.name: Nausea
REMEDIES.summary: A cooling lemon-honey sip to settle the stomach.
steps: Add the ingredients to 50 ml of water with 2 scoops honey.
Stir well.
Drink it.

REMEDIES.name: Joint Pain
REMEDIES.summary: An anti-inflammatory drink plus an oil massage.
REMEDIES.extra: a pinch of black salt; massage joints with Sarson oil (#26)
steps: Add the ingredients to 50 ml of water with a pinch of black salt; massage joints with Sarson oil (#26).
Stir well.
Drink it.

REMEDIES.name: Diarrhea
REMEDIES.summary: A binding spice-and-honey mixture.
warn: Replace lost fluids. Ongoing or severe diarrhea, or blood in stool, needs medical care.
steps: Combine the ground ingredients.
Mix with 2 scoops honey.
Take the mixture directly.

REMEDIES.name: Skin Rashes
REMEDIES.summary: A Tulsi paste applied to the skin.
steps: Mix into a paste with a little water.
Apply over the affected area.

REMEDIES.name: Stomach Ache
REMEDIES.summary: A warming spice drink with rock salt.
steps: Add the ingredients to 50 ml of water with 1 scoop rock salt.
Stir well.
Drink it.

REMEDIES.name: Tooth Ache
REMEDIES.summary: A clove paste applied to the aching tooth.
steps: Mix into a paste with a few drops of water.
Apply to the affected tooth with a cotton swab.

REMEDIES.name: Cuts & Wounds
REMEDIES.summary: A turmeric and aloe paste for minor wounds.
warn: For minor cuts only. Deep, dirty or heavily bleeding wounds need medical care.
steps: Mix into a paste with a little water.
Apply over the affected area.

REMEDIES.name: Burns
REMEDIES.summary: Aloe vera gel applied to the burn.
warn: First cool a burn under running water. Large, deep or blistering burns need medical care.
steps: Apply the fresh aloe gel liberally over the affected area.

REMEDIES.name: Ear Pain
REMEDIES.summary: A warm garlic-infused oil for the ear.
warn: Do not use if the eardrum may be perforated or there is discharge. See a doctor for severe or persistent ear pain.
steps: Heat the garlic in the mustard oil until golden brown.
Strain and let the oil cool to a safe, warm temperature.
Put a little of the oil into the affected ear.

REMEDIES.name: Hypertension (>150/100)
REMEDIES.summary: An Arjuna-hibiscus decoction for high blood pressure.
warn: Continue any prescribed blood-pressure medication. Monitor your blood pressure and consult your doctor.
steps: Add all ingredients to about 100 ml of water.
Boil for 5 minutes.
Strain and let it cool slightly.
Sip while warm.

REMEDIES.name: Diabetes (>250 mg/dl)
REMEDIES.summary: A spice decoction to support blood-sugar balance.
warn: Continue any prescribed diabetes medication and keep monitoring your blood sugar. Consult your doctor.
steps: Add all ingredients to about 100 ml of water.
Boil for 5 minutes.
Strain and let it cool slightly.
Sip while warm.

REMEDIES.name: Impotency / Low Sperm Count
REMEDIES.summary: A restorative blend taken with warm water.
steps: Combine the ingredients.
Take with 100 ml of warm water.

REMEDIES.name: Female Reproductive Health
REMEDIES.summary: A Shatavari decoction for women's health.
steps: Add all ingredients to about 100 ml of water.
Boil for 5 minutes.
Strain and let it cool slightly.
Sip while warm.

REMEDIES.name: Tooth Powder
REMEDIES.summary: A daily herbal tooth powder.
steps: Mix all ingredients into a fine powder with 1 scoop rock salt.
Massage onto the teeth and gums.

REMEDIES.name: Head Wash
REMEDIES.summary: A Triphala soak for the scalp and hair.
steps: Soak the ingredients overnight.
Apply to the scalp.
Leave for about half an hour.
Rinse away with water.

REMEDIES.name: Face Wash
REMEDIES.summary: A turmeric-aloe face mask.
steps: Mix into a smooth paste.
Apply on the face.
Leave for 5 minutes, then wash off.

REMEDIES.name: Heart Attack — First Aid
REMEDIES.summary: Cayenne first-aid measure while emergency help is on the way.
warn: CALL EMERGENCY SERVICES IMMEDIATELY. This is a folk first-aid measure only and is NOT a treatment for a heart attack.

ingredients.qty: 1 tsp (conscious) / a few drops of extract (unconscious)

steps: Call your local emergency number immediately.
If the person is conscious: give warm water mixed with 1 teaspoon of cayenne pepper (Lal Mirch, #28).
If the person is unconscious: place a few drops of cayenne extract under the tongue.
Stay with them until emergency help arrives.

EMERG.name: Chest Pain / Heart Attack
steps: Call your local emergency number now.
Have the person sit down, rest and stay calm; loosen tight clothing.
If they take prescribed heart medication (e.g. nitroglycerin), help them use it as directed.
If they become unresponsive and are not breathing normally, begin CPR and use an AED if available.
EMERG.codedesc: the source lists this as a code for myocardial infarction on the way to hospital

EMERG.name: Stroke (F.A.S.T.)
steps: Call emergency services immediately and note the time symptoms started.
Check F.A.S.T. — Face drooping, Arm weakness, Speech difficulty, Time to call.
Keep the person calm and still, lying with head and shoulders slightly raised.
Do not give food, drink or medication.
EMERG.codedesc: the source lists this for first aid in strokes on the way to hospital

EMERG.name: Severe Bleeding
steps: Call emergency services.
Apply firm, direct pressure on the wound with a clean cloth or dressing.
Do not remove a soaked cloth — add more on top and keep pressing.
Keep the injured area raised if possible and maintain pressure until help arrives.
EMERG.codedesc: the source lists this for hemostasis in hemorrhage while seeking care

EMERG.name: Breathing Difficulty
steps: Call emergency services.
Help the person sit upright and stay calm.
If they have a prescribed inhaler, help them use it.
Loosen tight clothing and keep the area well ventilated.
EMERG.codedesc: the source lists this for respiratory distress

EMERG.name: Poisoning
steps: Call emergency services or your poison control centre immediately.
Do NOT make the person vomit unless specifically told to.
Try to identify the substance and have the container ready.
If they are unconscious and not breathing, begin CPR.

EMERG.name: Burns
steps: Cool the burn under cool running water for at least 20 minutes.
Remove jewellery or tight items near the burn before it swells.
Cover loosely with a clean, non-stick dressing or cling film.
Do not apply ice, butter or pop blisters. Seek care for large, deep or facial burns.
EMERG.codedesc: the source lists this for burns and sunburn
